When calling `python`, emit exactly the tool-call format shown elsewhere in this prompt for this model. sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Looking at the ascii output, I see the player (NN) is at rows 38-39, cols 12-13. The gap between the lower and upper gray platforms has a pattern of b/c (blue/charcoal) at rows 34-37, cols 9-11. This looks like a ladder or climbable structure.
Let me try to move LEFT to get to the ladder area, then UP to climb it.
But wait - I need to first understand what the b/c pattern is. Looking at the pattern:
- Row 34: bcb (blue-charcoal-blue)
- Row 35: cbcb (charcoal-blue-charcoal-blue)
- Row 36: bcb (blue-charcoal-blue)
- Row 37: cbcb (charcoal-blue-charcoal-blue)
This looks like a diagonal/ladder pattern. The blue pixels form a diagonal staircase. Let me try moving LEFT to get to the ladder, then UP.
Actually, looking more carefully at the segmentation and the ascii, the player needs to navigate to reach the colored blocks above. Let me try moving LEFT first to position under the ladder, then UP.
The ladder appears to be at cols 9-11. The player is at cols 12-13. Let me move LEFT to get to col 10-11 area, then try UP.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Look at the ladder area more precisely
lines = current_frame.ascii.split('\n')
print("Ladder area (cols 8-14, rows 33-40):")
for r in range(33, 41):
    if r < len(lines):
        print(f"Row {r}: '{lines[r][8:14]}'")

# The player is at cols 12-13, need to move LEFT to get to the ladder at cols 9-11
# Let's try moving LEFT then UP
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

sections.content:

- Row 33: ggggcc (upper gray platform ends at col 11)
- Rows 34-37: ladder pattern (b/c alternating) at cols 8-11
- Row 38-39: ggggNN (lower gray platform with player)
The ladder is at cols 8-11, rows 34-37. The player is at cols 12-13. To climb the ladder, I need to:
1. Move LEFT to get to the ladder area (cols 10-11)
2. Then move UP to climb
Let me move LEFT first, then try UP.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:

<parameter=code>

action(['LEFT'])
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
